g.text: How do two isolated JS contexts coordinate securely?
A web page running inside VS Code.
from the UI, fetches data from APIs
Both sides send JSON messages like:
provider.ts has a switch/case that routes
each of 17 commands to the right handler.
The handler calls APIs or reads files,
then sends a typed response back.
It's like a mini REST API — but messages
go through VS Code, not the internet.
Why can't the webview fetch data?
Problem: The webview runs in a locked-
Solution: The webview sends a message
to the extension, the extension makes
the API call, and sends the data back.
All data is proxied through the host.
User clicks skill A, then quickly clicks B.
Both API requests are in-flight at once.
A counter increments on each request.
Before sending any response, the handler
checks: "Am I still the latest request?"
If not, the old response is silently dropped.
One number prevents the wrong skill's
